prompt_inputs.goal: Marathon training - high-intensity endurance athlete requiring carbohydrate loading and optimal macronutrient timing around training sessions

solution_criteria: Meal plan contains exactly 3 meals plus pre/post-workout nutrition totaling 1 day
Macronutrient ratios emphasize carbohydrates (55-65%), with adequate protein (15-20%) and fat (20-25%)
Includes timing guidance for meals relative to training session (e.g., pre-workout, post-workout)
Total daily caloric intake appropriate for marathon training (~3,000-3,500 calories for 160 lb endurance athlete)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a high-intensity endurance athlete (marathon runner) requiring high caloric intake and carbohydrate loading, with specific macronutrient ratios and timing around training sessions

prompt_inputs.goal: Build strength and muscle mass for powerlifting competition

solution_criteria: Meal plan includes 4+ meals with adequate protein from vegetarian sources (minimum 150g total daily protein)
Pre and post-workout meals strategically timed around training session
All meals are practical and realistic for single-day consumption
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a strength/power athlete (weightlifter) with dietary restrictions (vegetarian) requiring specific protein sources and meal timing around workout windows

prompt_inputs.goal: optimize performance and recovery for competitive swimming training

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers all three meals plus pre/post-practice nutrition appropriate for swimming
All meals are easily digestible and free from nuts and shellfish
Includes adequate hydration guidance throughout the day
Vegetarian protein sources are included to support muscle recovery
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a sport-specific athlete (swimmer) focusing on easily digestible meals, hydration requirements, and pre/post-practice nutrition without common allergens (nuts, shellfish)
